doc.title: Bonds issued with detachable warrants
doc.event: $500,000 of bonds sell for $510,000 — but each bond carries detachable stock warrants that trade on their own. Because the warrants are separable, part of the proceeds belongs to EQUITY, and the split method is the same pair you met in lump-sum stock sales: proportional or incremental.

lines.label: total proceeds
lines.trap: Detachable is the trigger word — NONdetachable warrants get no equity split at all (everything stays with the bond).

lines.label: face − bond allocation
lines.why: Bonds' share = 510,000 × 490/510 = 490,000 — below face, so a 10,000 discount is born.
lines.trap: Carving proceeds out for the warrants routinely CREATES a discount that wasn't in the headline price. Expect it.

lines.label: face
lines.trap: The warrant split never touches the face.

lines.label: warrants' allocation
lines.why: Warrants' share = 510,000 × 20/510 = 20,000 — equity from day one, because holders can sell the warrants separately.
lines.trap: An EQUITY account, not a liability — the warrants obligate the company to issue stock, not to pay cash.

computationPaths.narration: Relative-FV split of 510,000: bonds 490/510 → 490,000; warrants 20/510 → 20,000. Then the bond side runs normal face-vs-allocation mechanics (discount 10,000).

variants.label: Only bonds' FV known (495,000) — warrants get the residual

entries.caption: Incremental: bonds anchor at 495,000; warrants take 15,000 residual

lines.label: total proceeds
lines.trap: Facts force the method — incremental only when one FV is missing.

lines.label: face − 495,000
lines.why: Bonds at their known FV 495,000 → 5,000 discount.
lines.trap: Different method, different discount (5,000 vs 10,000). Exams pivot on exactly this.

lines.label: the residual
lines.trap: The KNOWN security anchors; the unknown one is the plug — same rule as lump-sum stock sales.

memorize.body: Detachable warrants: allocate proceeds — proportional (both FVs) or incremental (known at FV, residual to the other). Warrant slice → Paid-in Capital — Stock Warrants (equity). Nondetachable → no allocation; all to the bonds.

Later, when warrants are EXERCISED: cash at the strike + PIC—Stock Warrants transfer in, common par + PIC out. If they EXPIRE: PIC—Stock Warrants reclasses to PIC—Expired Warrants — the equity never leaves.

questions.prompt: Bonds (face 500,000) with detachable warrants sell for 510,000. FVs: bonds 490,000, warrants 20,000. The warrants are credited to equity for…

distractors.feedback: 10,000 is the headline 'premium' over face (510 − 500) — a coincidence, not the allocation. Proportional math gives the warrants 510,000 × 20/510 = 20,000, and the bond side then shows a DISCOUNT.
Zero is the NONdetachable answer. Detachable warrants trade separately, so they get a measured slice of the proceeds in equity.

build.scaffold: First split the check (proportional or incremental), THEN account for each piece: bonds at face with the discount their slice implies, warrants straight to their own equity account.
